title: The Darkness Before Them
author: Matthew Ward
prompt:
Question: Which of the following passages is verbatim from the "The Darkness Before Them" book by Matthew Ward?
Options:
A. Soon only Yennika remained in the circle, her eyes downcast. “Mirela’s wrong, Ihsan. All she’s doing is thinning our forces. If Vallant  does  strike along the Kemmuz Valley, so what? There’s always a harvest surplus elsewhere. It will cost us a slice of Salenna’s profits, but we’ll survive that. If something goes wrong on the Copper Road with the Eternity King’s legions on the march…? One of us is sure to be summoned to Zariqaz, and I daresay we won’t return.” She took a deep breath and drew back her shoulders, the determination Damant was learning to appreciate – even admire – returning to the cast of her brow. “Aunt Elinor placed me in command of the custodium. The decision is mine, not Mirela’s.” 
B. Before long just Yennika stayed in the circle, her eyes downcast. "Mirela's mistaken, Ihsan. All she's doing is thinning our forces. If Vallant attacks along the Kemmuz Valley, so what? There will always be a surplus elsewhere. It will cost us some of Salenna's profits, but we'll get by. If something goes wrong on the Copper Road while the Eternity King's legions are marching...? One of us will surely be summoned to Zariqaz, and I expect we won't come back." She took a deep breath and pulled back her shoulders, the determination Damant was starting to appreciate - even admire - returning to her face. "Aunt Elinor made me head of the custodium. It's my call, not Mirela's."  
C. Soon only Yennika was left in the circle, her eyes downcast. "Mirela's wrong, Ihsan. All she's doing is weakening our forces. If Vallant attacks along the Kemmuz Valley, so what? There will still be a surplus elsewhere. We'll take a hit to Salenna's profits but we'll survive. If something goes wrong on the Copper Road while the Eternity King's legions are on the move...? One of us will surely be summoned to Zariqaz, and I expect we won't come back." She took a deep breath and straightened her shoulders, the determination Damant was starting to appreciate - even admire - returning to her face. "Aunt Elinor put me in charge of the custodium. It's my decision, not Mirela's."
D. Soon only Yennika lingered in the circle, her eyes downcast. "Mirela's wrong, Ihsan. All she's doing is weakening our forces. If Vallant attacks along the Kemmuz Valley, no matter. There's always a surplus elsewhere. It will cost us some of Salenna's profits, but we'll manage. If something goes amiss on the Copper Road while the Eternity King's legions are on the move...? One of us will surely be summoned to Zariqaz, and I expect we won't come back." She took a deep breath and pulled back her shoulders, the determination Damant was learning to appreciate - even admire - returning to her expression. "Aunt Elinor made me head of the custodium. It's my decision, not Mirela's."
Answer:
